channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah
item.description: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.
item.content:encoded: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Historian shares how Russia benefits from the US being at war with Iran
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles diagnosed with breast cancer
item.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with breast cancer, President Trump said Monday.
item.content:encoded: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with breast cancer, President Trump said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: How Trump's Treasury is shifting sanctions to punish his critics and reward friends
item.description: Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.
item.content:encoded:
Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras, Evaristo Sa and Bastien Ohier/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: How the energy crisis fueled by the war in Iran is affecting Asia
item.description: Asia is feeling the energy shock of the war on Iran. NPR's A Martinez talks to Sam Reynolds of the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis.
item.content:encoded: Asia is feeling the energy shock of the war on Iran. NPR's A Martinez talks to Sam Reynolds of the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel's ground moves in Lebanon raise pressure on Western countries, Europeans leaders reject Trump's demand to help with the Strait of Hormuz, blackout leaves millions without power in Cuba.
item.content:encoded: Israel's ground moves in Lebanon raise pressure on Western countries, Europeans leaders reject Trump's demand to help with the Strait of Hormuz, blackout leaves millions without power in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies
item.description: A federal judge has issued a preliminary ruling that puts a hold on the Trump administration's overhaul of vaccine policies, including cuts to the number of recommended vaccines for children.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge has issued a preliminary ruling that puts a hold on the Trump administration's overhaul of vaccine policies, including cuts to the number of recommended vaccines for children.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Many say they use cannabis for their mental health, but there's no evidence it helps
item.description: Many people say they use cannabis for their mental health, but researchers find there is little to no evidence that cannabis can be beneficial for any psychiatric condition.
item.content:encoded: Many people say they use cannabis for their mental health, but researchers find there is little to no evidence that cannabis can be beneficial for any psychiatric condition.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Far from home, millions of migrant workers in the Gulf are trapped by war
item.description: These foreign nationals are the silent engines that keep many of the region's cities running, but the war has exposed some of their biggest vulnerabilities.
item.content:encoded:
These foreign nationals are the silent engines that keep many of the region's cities running, but the war has exposed some of their biggest vulnerabilities.

(Image credit: Giuseppe Cacace / AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Westerman

item.title: Kennedy Center board votes to close venue for two years for renovations
item.description: The Kennedy Center board voted Monday to close the complex for renovations. The vote follows President Trump's announcement that he plans to replace the venue's president Richard Grenell.
item.content:encoded: The Kennedy Center board voted Monday to close the complex for renovations. The vote follows President Trump's announcement that he plans to replace the venue's president Richard Grenell.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas
